plan.md.text: regarding 'SOURCE (file / http / channel / wire)', I feel like channel should somehow the top, and then others below, because http ultimately uses channel, right? same with file, right?
plan.md.author: user

plan.md.text: Fixed. Redrew the target-shape diagram with channel as the foundation — file / http / stream are channel kinds below it, channel.read is the one boundary, and 'wire' isn't a peer source, it's the case where the channel's Mime is the wire format. Part 4 now makes file and http channel kinds (app/channel/file/, app/channel/http/) that read through channel.read instead of doing their own I/O. You're right that both ultimately go through channel. Also added a 'Code file structure' section with the resulting tree + a write→read symmetry map.
plan.md.author: architect
